analysis.executive_summary: Documentation audit of Inngest covered 628 pages across 858 discovered URLs, achieving 100% crawl coverage with 91.5% overall confidence. API reference coverage is critically low at 19.51% despite 348 API pages detected, indicating significant gaps in usage documentation. Freshness metadata is present on only 9.71% of pages, severely limiting change tracking capability. Code example reliability is strong at 92.85%, and link health is excellent with only 1 confirmed broken internal link.
strengths: Complete crawl coverage at 100% with 628 pages successfully indexed
Excellent link health with only 1 broken internal documentation link out of 858 URLs examined
Strong code example reliability at 92.85%, indicating well-maintained sample code
High overall audit confidence at 91.5% with strong crawl and link validation metrics
Low SEO/geo issue rate at 3.82%, suggesting good technical SEO foundation
risks: Critical API coverage gap: only 19.51% of 348 detected API pages have corresponding usage documentation
Severe freshness tracking limitation with 9.71% last-updated coverage prevents change management and staleness detection
Large API surface area (348 pages) creates high maintenance burden without adequate usage guidance
Missing freshness metadata blocks customer ability to identify recently updated or deprecated content
Low API coverage may force customers to rely on raw reference docs without implementation context
Absence of temporal metadata prevents documentation aging analysis and proactive refresh cycles
Single broken link may indicate broader content maintenance gaps not yet surfaced

prioritized_actions.action: Implement systematic last-updated timestamps across all 628 pages to enable freshness tracking and content lifecycle management
Create usage documentation and integration guides for the 280+ API endpoints currently lacking coverage (target 80%+ coverage)
Establish automated freshness monitoring and quarterly review process for API documentation given large surface area
Fix the 1 confirmed broken internal link and implement automated link checking in CI/CD pipeline
Audit the 348 API pages to identify highest-traffic endpoints and prioritize usage doc creation for top 20%

limitations: External audit cannot verify accuracy or completeness of technical content, code examples, or API specifications
Freshness analysis severely limited by 9.71% timestamp coverage; actual content staleness cannot be assessed for 90%+ of pages
API coverage detection relies on structural patterns; actual API completeness requires internal schema validation
Cannot assess documentation quality, clarity, or alignment with product features and customer workflows
Link validation represents point-in-time check; dynamic content, authentication-gated pages, and external link stability not continuously monitored
